MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS
Off Calendar Sep-10-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES VIA ZOOM VIDEOCONFERENCE:
Barzelay, Jeanette for Plaintiff.
Johnson, Betsy for Defendant.

ARGUED (as fully reported in the reporter's transcript). The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument adopts the tentative ruling as follows:

DEFENDANT ZUME, INC.'s MOTION TO STAY PROCEEDINGS:

Defendant and Cross-Complainant Zume, Inc.'s motion to stay is granted. Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4 provides that if a court has ordered arbitration of a controversy which is an issue involved in an action or proceeding before a court, the court in which the action or proceeding is pending "shall, upon motion of a party to such action or proceeding, stay the action or proceeding until an arbitration is had . . . ." (Code Civ. Proc. 1281.4.) Even where no order or application for an order compelling arbitration has been made (here, because the parties voluntarily submitted to contractual arbitration), "it is within the implicit power of the court to stay the legal proceedings while arbitration is proceeding." (Seidman & Seidman v. Wolfson (1975) 50 Cal.App.3d 826, 834.) Here, as in Seidman & Seidman, "although no order or application for order have been made, it would be a useless act to require [Zume] to submit such an application when the arbitration is already well under way." (Id.) "The purpose of the statutory stay [required by section 1281.4] is to protect the jurisdiction of the arbitrator by preserving the status quo until the arbitration is resolved. In the absence of a stay, the continuation of the proceedings in the trial disrupts the arbitration proceedings and can render them ineffective." (Federal Ins. Co. v. Superior Court (1998) 60 Cal.App.4th 1370, 1374-1375.) Even if the pending arbitration does not encompass all of the issues as between the Plaintiffs/Cross-Defendants and Zume, even "a single overlapping issue sufficient to require imposition of a stay." (Heritage Provider Network, Inc. v. Superior Court (2008) 158 Cal.App.4th 1146, 1153.) Here, Zume's arbitration demand and the Pivot Sellers' contingent counterclaim in arbitration raise overlapping issues, including whether Zume owes Plaintiffs any earnout payments under the Membership Interest Purchase Agreement and whether it is entitled to an offset against such payments for the costs of indemnification against a third-party claim.

order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman, Clerk: S. Le, Court Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR#8600, maria.torreano@gmail.com, Reported. =(302/EPS)
